steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question asking about the relationship between Harding and Zorya. The passage's final sentence is the decisive clue: it says that Harding and others like her helped make it possible for more women, including Maya Zorya, to enter the field of cryptology. Harding is an early contributor; Zorya is a current practitioner in the same field.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Eleanor Mae Harding taught Maya Zorya how to securely manage important data.’: The passage never says Harding taught Zorya directly. Harding's contributions were administrative and award-winning, not described as personal instruction to any individual. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Eleanor Mae Harding and Maya Zorya worked together early in their careers.’: The passage gives no indication their careers overlapped in time; Harding is presented as an earlier pioneer and Zorya as a current practitioner. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Eleanor Mae Harding founded an organization that Maya Zorya currently works for.’: The passage names the NSA and the National Cryptologic School as organizations Harding worked within, not ones she founded — and nothing links Zorya to either institution. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Eleanor Mae Harding made earlier contributions to a field that Maya Zorya currently works within.’: This matches the passage exactly: Harding's earlier contributions helped open the field of cryptology, and Zorya currently works and teaches within it. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
